nodes.name: Opera Essentials
nodes.description: Famous operas and the composers who wrote them

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera (1896) set in 1830s Paris about the love story of seamstress Mimì and poet Rodolfo, later the basis for the musical *Rent*?
items.explanation:
Opera in four acts, premiered at the Teatro Regio in Turin on February 1, 1896, conducted by a 28-year-old Arturo Toscanini
Set in the Latin Quarter of 1830s Paris among struggling bohemian artists
Famous arias include 'Che gelida manina' (Rodolfo) and 'Sì, mi chiamano Mimì' (Mimì)
Jonathan Larson's musical *Rent* (1996) transposed the story to 1990s New York City's East Village
Mimì dies of tuberculosis in the final act — one of opera's most famous death scenes
Competed directly with Ruggero Leoncavallo's opera of the same name; the rival version is now forgotten

items.prompt: Which French composer wrote the opera (1875) set in Seville about a free-spirited gypsy who seduces soldier Don José, featuring the 'Habanera' and 'Toreador Song'?
items.explanation:
Opéra comique in four acts, premiered at the Opéra-Comique in Paris on March 3, 1875
Set in and around Seville, Spain; the title character is a Roma cigarette factory worker who seduces the soldier Don José
The 'Habanera' ('L'amour est un oiseau rebelle') and 'Toreador Song' are among the most recognized melodies in all opera
Initially shocked audiences with its themes of seduction, jealousy, and murder on the operatic stage
The composer died on June 3, 1875 — exactly three months after the premiere — believing the work was a failure
Now the fourth most-performed opera worldwide and a staple of every major opera house

items.prompt: Which Austrian composer wrote the Singspiel (1791) featuring the Queen of the Night's aria with its famous high F6, his last completed opera, premiered two months before his death?
items.explanation:
Singspiel (German-language opera with spoken dialogue) in two acts, premiered September 30, 1791, at the Theater auf der Wieden in Vienna
The Queen of the Night's aria 'Der Hölle Rache' reaches high F6 multiple times, making it one of the most technically demanding soprano arias
Libretto by Emanuel Schikaneder, who also played Papageno in the premiere
Blends Masonic symbolism, fairy-tale elements, and Enlightenment ideals
The composer died on December 5, 1791, roughly two months after the premiere
Remains one of the most frequently performed operas in the world, popular with both newcomers and aficionados

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera (1904) set in Nagasaki about a geisha abandoned by an American naval officer, featuring the aria 'Un bel dì vedremo'?
items.explanation:
Opera originally in two acts; after the disastrous La Scala premiere, revised into three acts (the standard version today), set in Nagasaki, Japan, around 1900
Geisha Cio-Cio-San (Butterfly) marries American naval lieutenant Pinkerton, who abandons her; she waits three years for his return
'Un bel dì vedremo' ('One fine day') is one of the most beloved soprano arias in the repertoire
Disastrously flopped at its La Scala premiere on February 17, 1904; the composer revised it to great success three months later at Brescia
Based on a short story by John Luther Long and a play by David Belasco
Ends with Butterfly's suicide by her father's dagger upon learning Pinkerton has returned with an American wife

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera (1871) set in ancient Egypt, commissioned for the Khedivial Opera House in Cairo, featuring a famous triumphal march?
items.explanation:
Opera in four acts, commissioned by Khedive Ismail for the Khedivial Opera House in Cairo (the house had opened in 1869 with *Rigoletto*; *Aida* premiered two years later)
Premiered in Cairo on December 24, 1871; the composer refused to attend, disliking sea voyages
Set in ancient Egypt during a war between Egypt and Ethiopia; the title character is an Ethiopian princess enslaved in Egypt
The Act II triumphal march ('Gloria all'Egitto') is one of the most spectacular scenes in opera
Love triangle between the Egyptian princess Amneris, the enslaved Aida, and the Egyptian military commander Radames
Aida and Radames are entombed alive together in the final scene while Amneris prays above

items.prompt: Which German composer created a cycle of four operas totaling roughly 15 hours (1869–1876), based on Norse mythology, for which he built a custom theater at Bayreuth?
items.explanation:
Four interconnected operas: *Das Rheingold*, *Die Walküre*, *Siegfried*, and *Götterdämmerung*
Totals approximately 15 hours of music; took 26 years to complete (1848–1874)
The Bayreuth Festspielhaus was purpose-built in 1876 for the complete cycle's premiere and still hosts an annual festival exclusively devoted to this composer's works
Draws on Norse/Germanic mythology: the Nibelungs, Valkyries, the cursed ring of the Rhinegold, and the twilight of the gods
Pioneered the use of leitmotifs — recurring musical themes associated with specific characters, objects, or ideas
Performing the complete cycle is a prestige event for opera companies; audiences attend over four consecutive evenings
items.cardFront: Der Ring des Nibelungen (The Ring Cycle)

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera buffa (1816) in which the barber Figaro helps Count Almaviva woo Rosina, featuring Figaro's famous rapid-fire entrance aria?
items.explanation:
Opera buffa (comic opera) in two acts, premiered at the Teatro Argentina in Rome on February 20, 1816
'Largo al factotum' (Figaro's entrance aria, with its repeated 'Figaro! Figaro! Figaro!') is one of the most recognized arias in opera
Flopped spectacularly on opening night due to sabotage by fans of Giovanni Paisiello's older version of the same story; recovered within days
Based on the first play in Pierre Beaumarchais's Figaro trilogy; the second play became the basis for a different composer's *The Marriage of Figaro*
Composed in just two to three weeks, typical of the composer's legendary speed
The character of Figaro — a clever, scheming barber — has become an archetype in Western culture

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera (1853) about a Parisian courtesan who renounces love, based on Dumas' *La Dame aux camélias* and featuring the famous 'Brindisi' drinking song?
items.explanation:
Opera in three acts, premiered at La Fenice in Venice on March 6, 1853
Title means 'The Fallen Woman'; based on Alexandre Dumas fils' novel and play *La Dame aux camélias*
The courtesan Violetta renounces her lover Alfredo at his father's insistence, then dies of tuberculosis
The 'Brindisi' ('Libiamo ne' lieti calici') is one of the most famous drinking songs in opera
The premiere was a fiasco — the audience laughed at the overweight soprano dying of consumption; revised productions triumphed
Consistently among the top three most-performed operas worldwide alongside *La Bohème* and *Carmen*

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera (1900) set during Napoleon's 1800 Italian campaign, ending with the heroine leaping from the Castel Sant'Angelo after stabbing the villain Baron Scarpia?
items.explanation:
Opera in three acts, premiered at the Teatro Costanzi in Rome on January 14, 1900
Set in Rome on June 17–18, 1800, during the Napoleonic Wars; action spans less than 24 hours
Three principal characters: the singer Tosca, her lover the painter Cavaradossi, and the corrupt police chief Baron Scarpia
'Vissi d'arte' ('I lived for art') is Tosca's famous aria lamenting her fate
Ends with Tosca stabbing Scarpia, Cavaradossi's execution by firing squad, and Tosca leaping to her death from the battlements of the Castel Sant'Angelo
Sometimes called a 'shabby little shocker' (a phrase attributed to musicologist Joseph Kerman), yet it remains one of the most gripping works in the repertoire

items.prompt: Which Austrian composer wrote the 'dramma giocoso' (1787) about a legendary seducer who is ultimately dragged to hell by the stone statue of a man he murdered?
items.explanation:
Dramma giocoso (a blend of comic and serious opera) in two acts, premiered in Prague on October 29, 1787
Based on the legend of Don Juan, the compulsive seducer; Leporello's 'Catalogue Aria' lists 2,065 conquests across Europe
The stone statue of the murdered Commendatore comes to dinner and drags Don Giovanni to hell in the finale
Libretto by Lorenzo Da Ponte, who also wrote *The Marriage of Figaro* and *Così fan tutte* for the same composer
Often considered the greatest opera ever written, blending comedy, tragedy, and the supernatural
Søren Kierkegaard devoted a major section of *Either/Or* (1843) to analyzing this work as the supreme expression of the aesthetic life

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera (1851) about a hunchback court jester whose daughter is seduced by a Duke, featuring the tenor aria 'La donna è mobile'?
items.explanation:
Opera in three acts, premiered at La Fenice in Venice on March 11, 1851
Based on Victor Hugo's play *Le roi s'amuse*; Austrian censors forced changes to the setting (from the French court to Mantua)
The hunchback jester Rigoletto tries to protect his daughter Gilda from the lecherous Duke of Mantua
'La donna è mobile' ('Woman is fickle') is one of the most famous tenor arias; the composer kept it secret until opening night to prevent it from leaking
Ends tragically with Rigoletto discovering that the body in the sack he ordered killed is his own daughter, not the Duke
Part of the composer's 'popular trilogy' along with *Il trovatore* (1853) and *La traviata* (1853)

items.prompt: Which Austrian composer wrote the opera (1786) in which a clever servant prevents his master from exercising 'droit du seigneur,' with a libretto by Lorenzo Da Ponte?
items.explanation:
Opera buffa in four acts, premiered at the Burgtheater in Vienna on May 1, 1786
Based on the second play in Beaumarchais's Figaro trilogy, following the story continued from *The Barber of Seville*
Figaro outmaneuvers Count Almaviva, who is trying to exercise the feudal 'droit du seigneur' with Figaro's bride Susanna
Libretto by Lorenzo Da Ponte; Beaumarchais's play was banned in Vienna for its anti-aristocratic themes, requiring careful adaptation
The Act III sextet and Act IV finale are considered among the greatest ensemble scenes in opera history
A modest success in Vienna but a sensation in Prague, where it ran for months and led to the commission for *Don Giovanni*
items.cardFront: The Marriage of Figaro (Le nozze di Figaro)

items.prompt: Which Italian composer left his final opera unfinished at his death in 1924, a work set in China whose aria 'Nessun dorma' was popularized by Luciano Pavarotti?
items.explanation:
Opera in three acts, left unfinished at the composer's death on November 29, 1924; completed by Franco Alfano
Set in legendary Peking (Beijing); Princess Turandot poses three riddles to suitors — failure means death
'Nessun dorma' ('None shall sleep') became globally famous when Luciano Pavarotti performed it at the 1990 FIFA World Cup
At the premiere on April 25, 1926, conductor Arturo Toscanini stopped at the point where the composer's manuscript ended and told the audience, 'Here the opera finishes, because at this point the Maestro died'
The slave girl Liù's self-sacrifice is the emotional heart of the opera; it was the last music the composer completed
The three riddles (Hope, Blood, Turandot) and the answer to the final riddle (the prince's name, Calaf) are central to the plot

items.prompt: Which Italian composer wrote the opera (1831) about a Druid priestess torn between sacred duty and love for a Roman proconsul, featuring the demanding soprano aria 'Casta diva'?
items.explanation:
Opera in two acts, premiered at La Scala in Milan on December 26, 1831
The Druid high priestess Norma secretly loves the Roman proconsul Pollione, by whom she has two children, violating her vows
'Casta diva' ('Chaste goddess'), a prayer to the moon, is one of the most technically demanding and celebrated soprano arias
Maria Callas made this her signature role, performing it 89 times and defining the modern standard
The bel canto style demands extreme vocal control: long legato lines, ornamentation, and a range spanning over two octaves
Ends with Norma confessing her violation and walking into a sacrificial pyre; Pollione, moved by her courage, joins her

items.prompt: Which German composer wrote the opera (1843) about a ghost ship captain cursed to sail forever unless redeemed by a faithful woman, based on a maritime legend?
items.explanation:
Opera in three acts, premiered at the Royal Court Theatre in Dresden on January 2, 1843
Based on the legend of a spectral ship doomed to sail the seas eternally; the captain can come ashore once every seven years to seek a faithful wife
Senta, a Norwegian woman obsessed with the legend, pledges her faithfulness and ultimately leaps into the sea to redeem him
One of the composer's earliest major operas, marking the transition from conventional opera toward his mature style
The overture depicts the storm-tossed sea and the Dutchman's curse through dramatic orchestration
Originally conceived as a one-act opera but expanded to three acts for the 1843 premiere
